title: cyathea lewisii
intro: Cyathea lewisii, also known as the Lewis's tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Cyathea lewisii can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
